# aiohttp_security

```{image} https://travis-ci.com/aio-libs/aiohttp-security.svg?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.com/aio-libs/aiohttp-security
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/github/aio-libs/aiohttp-security/coverage.svg?branch=master
:target: https://codecov.io/github/aio-libs/aiohttp-security
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/aiohttp-security/badge/?version=latest
:target: https://aiohttp-security.readthedocs.io/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/aiohttp-security.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/aiohttp-security
```

The library provides identity and authorization for [aiohttp.web](target-1).

## Installation

Simplest case (authorization via cookies)

```
$ pip install aiohttp_security
```

With `aiohttp-session` support

```
$ pip install aiohttp_security[session]
```

## Examples

Take a look at examples:

[Basic example]

[Example with DB auth]

and demos at **demo** directory.

## Documentation

<https://aiohttp-security.readthedocs.io/>

## Develop

`pip install -r requirements-dev.txt`

## License

`aiohttp_security` is offered under the Apache 2 license.

### CHANGES

% towncrier release notes start

# 0.5.0 (2023-11-18)

- Added type annotations.
- Added a reason message when permission is rejected.
- Switched to `aiohttp.web.AppKey`.
- Reverted change in `JWTIdentityPolicy` so identity returns `str`.

# 0.4.0 (2018-09-27)

- Bump minimal supported `aiohttp` version to 3.2.
- Use `request.config_dict` for accessing `jinja2` environment. It
  allows to reuse jinja rendering engine from parent application.

# 0.3.0 (2018-09-06)

- Deprecate `login_required` and `has_permission` decorators.
  Use `check_authorized` and `check_permission` helper functions instead.
- Bump supported `aiohttp` version to 3.0+.
- Enable strong warnings mode for test suite, clean-up all deprecation warnings.
- Polish documentation

# 0.2.0 (2017-11-17)

- Add `is_anonymous`, `login_required`, `has_permission` helpers. (#114)

# 0.1.2 (2017-10-17)

- Make aiohttp-session optional dependency. (#107)

[aiohttp_web]: http://aiohttp.readthedocs.org/en/latest/web.html

[example with db auth]: docs/example_db_auth.rst

functions.name: demo.database_auth.main.init_db
documentation: Initialise DB with sample data.

classes.name: demo.database_auth.db.User
documentation: A user and their credentials.

classes.name: demo.database_auth.db.Permission
documentation: A permission that grants a user access to something.

methods.name: demo.dictionary_auth.authz.DictionaryAuthorizationPolicy.authorized_userid
documentation:
Retrieve authorized user id.
Return the user_id of the user identified by the identity
or 'None' if no user exists related to the identity.

methods.name: demo.dictionary_auth.authz.DictionaryAuthorizationPolicy.permits
documentation:
Check user permissions.
Return True if the identity is allowed the permission in the
current context, else return False.

modules.name: aiohttp_security.cookies_identity
documentation:
Identity policy for storing info directly into HTTP cookie.

Use mostly for demonstration purposes, SessionIdentityPolicy is much
more handy.

classes.name: demo.simple_example_auth.SimpleJack_AuthorizationPolicy

methods.name: demo.simple_example_auth.SimpleJack_AuthorizationPolicy.authorized_userid
documentation:
Retrieve authorized user id.
Return the user_id of the user identified by the identity
or 'None' if no user exists related to the identity.

methods.name: demo.simple_example_auth.SimpleJack_AuthorizationPolicy.permits
documentation:
Check user permissions.
Return True if the identity is allowed the permission
in the current context, else return False.
Demo authorization policy for only one user.
User 'jack' has only 'listen' permission.
For more complicated authorization policies see examples
in the 'demo' directory.

modules.name: aiohttp_security.jwt_identity
documentation: Identity policy for storing info in the jwt token.

modules.name: aiohttp_security.session_identity
documentation:
Identity policy for storing info into aiohttp_session session.

aiohttp_session.setup() should be called on application initialization
to configure aiohttp_session properly.

classes.name: aiohttp_security.abc.AbstractIdentityPolicy

methods.name: aiohttp_security.abc.AbstractIdentityPolicy.identify
documentation:
Return the claimed identity of the user associated request or
`None` if no identity can be found associated with the request.

methods.name: aiohttp_security.abc.AbstractIdentityPolicy.remember
documentation:
Remember identity.

Modify response object by filling it's headers with remembered user.

An individual identity policy and its consumers can decide on
the composition and meaning of **kwargs.

methods.name: aiohttp_security.abc.AbstractIdentityPolicy.forget
documentation:
Modify response which can be used to 'forget' the
current identity on subsequent requests.
see http://plope.com/pyramid_auth_design_api_postmortem

methods.name: aiohttp_security.abc.AbstractAuthorizationPolicy.permits
documentation:
Check user permissions.

Return True if the identity is allowed the permission in the
current context, else return False.

methods.name: aiohttp_security.abc.AbstractAuthorizationPolicy.authorized_userid
documentation:
Retrieve authorized user id.

Return the user_id of the user identified by the identity
or 'None' if no user exists related to the identity.

functions.name: aiohttp_security.api.remember
documentation:
Remember identity into response.

The action is performed by identity_policy.remember()

Usually the identity is stored in user cookies somehow but may be
pushed into custom header also.

functions.name: aiohttp_security.api.forget
documentation:
Forget previously remembered identity.

Usually it clears cookie or server-side storage to forget user
session.

functions.name: aiohttp_security.api.is_anonymous
documentation:
Check if user is anonymous.

User is considered anonymous if there is not identity
in request.

functions.name: aiohttp_security.api.check_authorized
documentation: Checker that raises HTTPUnauthorized for anonymous users.

functions.name: aiohttp_security.api.check_permission
documentation:
Checker that passes only to authoraised users with given permission.

If user is not authorized - raises HTTPUnauthorized,
if user is authorized and does not have permission -
raises HTTPForbidden.
